title: allium subhirsutum subsp. album
intro: Allium subhirsutum subsp. album, also known as the white-woolly garlic, is a species of plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and strong garlic-like odor. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect against infections and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Allium subhirsutum subsp. album include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used fresh or dried, and can be consumed as a tea, infused in oils, or used in tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
